message:
fix(pager): prevent double-counting of cache misses in async load path

When an async page load completes, the code previously called
inner.request(&key) to check post-completion residency. However,
request() internally records a miss or ghost-hit if the key is not
found, duplicating the accounting already done during the initial
lookup. This caused the ARC adaptive parameter (p) to drift
incorrectly, biasing the cache toward one list.

The fix checks residency via get() first: if present (loaded by
another thread), we just touch LRU. If absent, we admit the page
using the original lookup classification without a redundant request()
call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
